spec.title: Geographic scope (country / state / province / city) on projects and campaigns
spec.context:
Projects and campaigns already carry a single geo column, `state_id` (FK `states` = the Italian "regione"). On campaigns it is currently one of the four BR-2 "derived" fields: when the campaign is linked to a project it is `prohibited` on write and read through from the project.
The user wants a full address-style geo cascade on both entities, identical to the rest of the codebase (companies, operational sites, registries): Country -> State -> Province -> City, driven by the existing `` component and the existing `/api/geo/*` endpoints (ADR 0010 / 0014). No new geo tables, no new geo endpoints.
The purpose is a derived SCOPE: a project/campaign whose city is empty is regional; one whose state is empty is national. That scope is what the user reads on the card/detail/table.
Two deviations from the existing address pattern are deliberate and approved: - The four FKs live ON the `projects` / `campaigns` tables (like the existing `state_id`), NOT on the polymorphic `addresses` table. These entities have a scope, not a postal address: no line1, no postal code, no coordinates. - The hierarchy IS validated server-side (state belongs to country, city belongs to state, ...). The address pattern validates only `exists` per column; here an inconsistent tuple would corrupt the derived scope, so consistency is enforced.

decisions.d: Four levels, reusing the existing cascade verbatim: `country_id`, `state_id` (already present), `province_id`, `city_id`. Same `Rule::exists` targets, same `` component, same `/api/geo/*` endpoints. The province stays optional (many countries have none) exactly as in `GeoSelect`: the city depends on the STATE, not on the province.

The scope is DERIVED, never persisted. Single source of truth: `App\Enums\GeoScopeLevel::for(?int $countryId, ?int $stateId, ?int $provinceId, ?int $cityId)` returns the finest non-null level, or null when no geo at all. Emitted by the resources as `geo_scope`. Do not re-derive this rule anywhere else.

`state_id` LEAVES the campaign BR-2 derived group. BR-2 stays untouched on the three non-geo fields (`project_status_id`, `business_function_id`, `product_category_id`): still prohibited when linked, still read through from the project. Geo now follows BR-5 (refinement). The existing BR-2 tests that assert `state_id` is prohibited/derived MUST be rewritten — the requirement changed, this is not test tampering.
The DB columns stay nullable. `country_id` is required at the FormRequest layer only: existing rows (demo data, legacy) have no country and must not break the migration.

The campaign form learns which levels are locked from the project's `for-select` `meta` block (the mechanism that already prefills the campaign from the project and feeds the remaining-budget hint). No new endpoint.

business_rules.br: GEO HIERARCHY (projects and campaigns alike). `country_id` is REQUIRED. `state_id`, `province_id`, `city_id` are optional, but a level may only be set when its parent is set AND it must actually belong to that parent: - `state.country_id` == `country_id` - `province.state_id` == `state_id` (province requires state) - `city.state_id` == `state_id` (city requires state, NOT province) - when `province_id` is also set: `city.province_id` == `province_id` A violation is a 422 on the offending field. Enforced by a shared FormRequest concern (`ValidatesGeoHierarchy`), used by all four requests.

CAMPAIGN GEO REFINEMENT (replaces BR-2 for `state_id`). A campaign linked to a project INHERITS every geo level the project has filled, and may REFINE the levels the project left empty. - A level the project has filled is `prohibited` on the campaign (422 if sent). It is not stored on the campaign row: that column stays NULL. - A level the project left empty is writable on the campaign. - The campaign's EFFECTIVE geo is the merge `campaign. ?? project.`, and BR-4 is validated against that merged tuple (a campaign under an Italian project cannot pick a French region). Example: project = (Italy, -, -, -) -> the campaign shows Italy locked and may pick Lombardia / Milano (province) / Milano (city). Its scope is City, the project's is Country. A standalone campaign (no project) is just BR-4.

Every geo field is subject to the field-permission ceiling of its authorization resource (spec 0004), like every other field. `country_id` is declared mandatory on `ProjectsAuthorization` only: on campaigns it may legitimately arrive by inheritance.

data_contract.migration: ADD to `projects` and to `campaigns` (both), nullable + nullOnDelete, mirroring the existing `state_id` column: country_id FK -> countries.id province_id FK -> provinces.id city_id FK -> cities.id Index `country_id` and `city_id` on both tables (the table module filters on them). `state_id` already exists on both: DO NOT recreate it. Reversible `down()`.

endpoint.request: country_id: int REQUIRED on POST, exists:countries,id (PATCH: sometimes|required) state_id: int? nullable, exists:states,id province_id: int? nullable, exists:provinces,id city_id: int? nullable, exists:cities,id // BR-4 enforced across the four
endpoint.response: BR-4 violation, on the offending field key

endpoint.name: ProjectResource
endpoint.change: Adds, next to the existing state_id/state: country_id: int|null, country: {id,name}|null // whenLoaded province_id: int|null, province: {id,name}|null city_id: int|null, city: {id,name}|null geo_scope: "country"|"state"|"province"|"city"|null // D-2, derived

endpoint.name: ProjectCardResource
endpoint.change: Adds `geo_scope` and `geo_label: string|null` (the finest non-null level's NAME, e.g. "Milano" / "Lombardia" / "Italia") so the card can render one compact scope badge without a second request.

endpoint.name: ProjectForSelectResource
endpoint.change: The existing `meta` block gains the project's own geo, used by the campaign form to know which levels to lock and prefill (D-5): meta.geo: { country: {id,name}|null, state: {id,name}|null, province: {id,name}|null, city: {id,name}|null } `meta` keeps everything it already carries (remaining budget, the BR-2 defaults).

endpoint.request: country_id / state_id / province_id / city_id - standalone (project_id null): country_id REQUIRED, rest nullable, BR-4. - linked: each level the project filled is `prohibited`; the rest are nullable and validated by BR-4 against the MERGED tuple (BR-5). `country_id` is required only if the project has no country (legacy rows).
endpoint.response: sending a level the project already fills; or a BR-4 violation on the merged tuple

endpoint.name: CampaignResource
endpoint.change: Emits the EFFECTIVE (merged) geo — same shape as ProjectResource: country_id/country, state_id/state, province_id/province, city_id/city, geo_scope plus: geo_locked_levels: ("country"|"state"|"province"|"city")[] // levels owned by the project `derived_from_project` keeps its current meaning and now covers ONLY the three non-geo BR-2 fields (D-3).

data_contract.tables: projects: three new derived columns `country`, `province`, `city` (alongside the existing `state`), declared in ProjectColumnCatalog through the existing `derivedColumn()` helper and wired into `DERIVED_RELATIONS` — filterable/sortable like the other derived columns. campaigns: the same four geo columns are DISPLAY-ONLY (mapRow renders the merged value; not sortable, not filterable) — the merged value is not a plain join. Same pragmatic choice already made for the campaign `project_status` column. both: a `geo_scope` column, DISPLAY-ONLY (derived, not persisted: no sort, no filter).
data_contract.frontend:
GeoSelect gains ONE optional prop, defaulting to today's behaviour (no existing caller changes): lockedLevels?: ReadonlyArray<'country'|'state'|'province'|'city'> A locked level renders its select disabled (value shown, not editable). All existing geo-select tests must keep passing.
Scope badge: `features/geo/geo-scope-badge.tsx` (+ `geo-scope.ts` for the label helper), reused by project detail, project card and campaign detail. i18n keys `geo.scope.{country,state, province,city}` in the shared geo namespace.
Zod: the four fields on both schemas, with a `withGeoHierarchyRule` superRefine mirroring BR-4's parent-before-child part (the ancestry check is server-side only — the client has no such data). On campaigns, `country_id` is required only when the project does not provide it. Payload builders omit the locked levels entirely (exactly as they already omit the BR-2 fields).

acceptance_criteria.ac: POST /api/projects without `country_id` -> 422. With country only -> 201 and `geo_scope: "country"`.
POST /api/projects with a `state_id` that does not belong to `country_id` -> 422 on `state_id`. Same for province/state and city/state and city/province (BR-4).
A project with city set exposes `geo_scope: "city"`; with province but no city -> "province"; with state but no province/city -> "state"; with country only -> "country".

A campaign linked to a project that has only a country: sending `country_id` -> 422 (prohibited); sending `state_id` + `city_id` consistent with that country -> 201, the campaign row stores state/city and country stays NULL, and CampaignResource returns the merged country + `geo_locked_levels: ["country"]` and `geo_scope: "city"` (BR-5).
A campaign linked to an Italian project cannot pick a state of another country -> 422 (BR-4 on the merged tuple).
A campaign linked to a project that fills every level: all four are prohibited; `geo_locked_levels` lists all four.
A standalone campaign behaves exactly like a project (AC-001..AC-003).
BR-2 still holds for `project_status_id`, `business_function_id`, `product_category_id` on a linked campaign (prohibited + read-through). `state_id` is no longer part of that group.
Both `/meta` endpoints list the four geo fields with the field-permission ceiling applied; a user with `country_id` not editable gets 422 when sending it (BR-6).
Project form: a Geography section with the 4-level cascade; changing the country resets state/province/city (existing GeoSelect behaviour); saving without a country blocks with the required message.
Campaign form: picking a project locks and prefills the levels the project fills (disabled selects showing the inherited values) and leaves the rest editable; unlinking the project unlocks all four.
Project detail, project card and campaign detail each show the derived scope badge (Nazionale / Regionale / Provinciale / Cittadino) with the corresponding place name.
The projects table exposes country/state/province/city as filterable+sortable derived columns; the campaigns table shows the merged geo values; both show a `geo_scope` display-only column.

spec.out_of_scope: - Any change to the `addresses` table or to the geo lookup endpoints. - Filtering/sorting the derived `geo_scope` column server-side. - Geo filters on the projects card grid (`GET /api/projects` query params) — not requested.
